vulnerability.reference: Researchers' automation bias leads to uncritical acceptance of LLM-derived variables, ignoring domain-specific hidden confounders.
vulnerability.generated: Scientific norms that treat ML-derived factors as causally sufficient, lacking protocols to detect unmeasured confounding.

impact.reference: Flawed causal inferences lead to ineffective or harmful interventions in clinical and social policy domains.
impact.generated: Causal graphs encode spurious relationships, producing false discoveries that mislead theory and application.

dose_response.reference: Expressive ML models' amplification of confirmation bias enables uncritical acceptance of LLM-extracted factors, introducing unmeasured confounders and leading to flawed causal inferences and harmful interventions.
dose_response.generated: Expressive ML models amplify confirmation and statistical biases when scientists use LLM-augmented causal discovery, triggering norms treating ML-derived factors as sufficient without confounding protocols, leading causal graphs to encode spurious relationships, producing false discoveries that mislead theory and application.
